title: Monteverdia haitiensis
taxonomy: Monteverdia haitiensis belongs to the family Celastraceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vine family. It is a member of the genus Monteverdia, which is a group of plants that are native to the Caribbean and Central America. The classification of Monteverdia haitiensis is further refined to the order Celastrales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their small flowers and distinctive fruit. The plant is also classified as a dicot, indicating that it has two cotyledons, or seed leaves, in its seedling stage. By understanding the classification of Monteverdia haitiensis, scientists can gain a better understanding of the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
uses: Monteverdia haitiensis is primarily found in the Caribbean region and has been utilized in traditional medicine for various purposes.
